channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Open Source DocSend Alternatives That Don
item.description: Every few months someone posts on Hacker News or Reddit asking "whats a good alternative to DocSend that wont cost me $50/user/month?" And every time, the thread fills up with people sharing the...

item.title: Final 3 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass until April 10, 11:59 p.m. PT. Secure your spot at the center of the tech ecosystem. Register here.

item.title: SonarQube Maven Plugin: Analyze Java Projects
item.description: The SonarQube Maven plugin is the most straightforward way to integrate static analysis into Java projects. Instead of installing a separate scanner binary and managing configuration files, the...

item.title: From AI Hype to Controlled Enterprise AI-Assisted Development

item.title: What is MCP? A Developer's Guide with Working Code
item.description: MCP stands for Model Context Protocol. It's an open standard that defines how AI models connect to external tools and data sources. Before MCP, every AI platform had its own way of doing tool...

item.title: I deployed a Python agent to the cloud in 45 seconds. Here's how.
item.description: Last week I built a small Python script that takes a URL and summarizes the page using Claude. Ran it locally, worked great. Then I needed to share it with a colleague who doesn't have Python...

item.title: Ollama, LM Studio, and GPT4All Are All Just llama.cpp — Here's Why Performance Still Differs

item.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI is in a relatively precarious position. The company is and has been a funding behemoth - just over a week ago, it closed $122 billion in funding at a post-money valuation of $852 billion....

item.title: Atlassian launches visual AI tools and third-party agents in Confluence
item.description: Confluence users can now create visual assets within the software in addition to new third-party agents working with Lovable, Replit, and Gamma.

item.title: Tractive’s new dog and cat trackers provide more health insights for your pets
item.description: After acquiring its competitor Whistle from Mars Petcare nearly a year ago, Tractive is introducing two new pet trackers today. The Cat 6 Mini features a sleeker design than Tractive's current...

item.title: Xbox’s new era needs games like Forza Horizon 6
item.description: It's a weird time at Xbox. Microsoft's gaming division is under new management, with its current leadership intent on what it describes as "a renewed commitment to Xbox." But given that no one...

item.title: The only USB-C AA battery I’d buy for myself is the Zepath 3600
item.description: Last September, a company named Lumafield scanned 1,000 cylindrical lithium-ion batteries to shine a light on the hidden risks of cheaping out. At roughly the same time, I found myself testing two...

item.title: Insta360’s Snap is a tiny magnetic phone screen for taking rear-camera selfies
item.description: Insta360 just announced the Snap, a new smartphone accessory designed to improve the quality of your selfies. It works like a digital mirror magnetically attached to the back of your Android or...

item.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
item.description: Unionized staff at ProPublica, one of the country's leading nonprofit newsrooms, are walking off the job for 24 hours beginning Wednesday and asking the public to honor a digital picket line. The...

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.description: The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of...

item.title: GoPro is cutting 23 percent of its workforce
item.description: GoPro has announced plans to cut nearly a quarter of its workforce in an effort to return to profitability, after struggling with increased competition and declining revenue. The cuts, announced...

item.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
item.description: End-of-life routers in homes and small offices hacked in 120 countries.

item.title: Continuous glucose monitoring made me continuously crazy
item.description: Daily life is different when you're tracking glucose. A little over a year ago, I was on my way to a conference. My bags were packed, the Uber was on its way, but there was one last thing to do...

item.title: Python Supply-Chain Compromise
item.description: This is news: A malicious supply chain compromise has been identified in the Python Package Index package litellm version 1.82.8. The published wheel contains a malicious .pth file...

item.title: Why Microsoft’s war on Windows’ Control Panel is taking so long
item.description: Microsoft first started trying to get rid of the Control Panel in 2012, with the launch of Windows 8. More than a decade later, it's still working on migrating all the old Control Panel items into...

item.title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known as Contagious Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's...

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

item.title: Introducing the Child Safety Blueprint
item.description: Discover OpenAI’s Child Safety Blueprint—a roadmap for building AI responsibly with safeguards, age-appropriate design, and collaboration to protect and empower young people online.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs),...
